summary: The paper has ZERO statistically significant results after proper multiple comparison correction (12 tests, Bonferroni alpha=0.00417, BH-FDR q<0.05). The sole uncorrected significant result (H1b L8: p=0.028) is post-hoc cherry-picked from H1 (the pre-registered primary hypothesis). The paper's framing of this as evidence of a real effect is methodologically incorrect. Key additional issues: (1) H9 co-occurrence measurement is a definitional tautology; (2) H6 primary hypothesis is decisively falsified (precision@20=0.0, Fisher p=1.0); (3) OrtSAE ablation comparison is at unmatched L0 (the confound the paper criticizes); (4) probe quality confound in CMI-absorption analysis (rho=-0.67); (5) post-hoc power analysis is methodologically questionable; (6) Feature U n=1 case overgeneralized; (7) MCC~0.21 across ALL variants including Random control suggests Hungarian matching is chance-level.

findings.description: Multiple comparisons problem: ZERO significant results after Bonferroni or BH-FDR correction. The paper claims H1b at layer 8 (Pearson p=0.028, Spearman p=0.009) as evidence, but these are post-hoc cherry-picks from H1 (the pre-registered primary hypothesis). After Bonferroni correction for 12 tests, the lowest corrected p is 0.107 (BH-FDR). The paper incorrectly presents the uncorrected p=0.028 as evidence of a real effect.
findings.suggestion: The paper must either: (a) honestly report zero significant results after correction and reframe the paper as a null-result study, or (b) pre-register H1b as the primary hypothesis before collecting data. The current framing is misleading.

findings.description: H9 co-occurrence measurement is a definitional tautology: p_11 + absorption = 1.0 by construction. The 'correlation' of r=-1.0 is mathematical, not empirical. This was known from iteration 1 but excluded from main paper.
findings.suggestion: Remove H9 from the paper entirely or clearly label it as a mathematical identity check, not an empirical finding.

findings.description: Primary hypothesis H6 is decisively falsified: precision@20=0.0, Fisher exact test p=1.0. The local inhibition graph does not predict any absorption pairs. The paper attempts to salvage this as a 'valuable negative result' but the title, abstract, introduction, and contributions all emphasize the graph as a predictive tool.
findings.suggestion: The paper cannot claim the graph is a contribution if it predicts nothing. Either drop the graph entirely and focus on the LCA theoretical contribution, or honestly reframe: 'The graph failed, but the theoretical framework is compelling.'

findings.description: OrtSAE ablation comparison is at unmatched L0 dimensions: OrtSAE without penalty (L0~920) vs OrtSAE with penalty (L0~550). The paper criticizes others for unmatched comparisons but makes the same mistake. The conclusion 'orthogonality penalty does not appear to reduce absorption' is invalid.
findings.suggestion: Either match L0 values or acknowledge this confound invalidates the comparison.

findings.description: Probe quality confound: absorption rate correlates with probe F1 at rho=-0.67 (p<0.001). CMI-absorption analysis uses rates from L0=82 where mean probe F1=0.817. Low-CMI letters may be inherently harder to probe, causing both low estimated CMI and artificially high absorption rates. Paper never computes partial correlation controlling for probe F1.
findings.suggestion: Compute partial correlation controlling for probe F1, or restrict CMI analysis to quality-gated letters (F1>0.85).

findings.description: CMI-absorption correlation is dimensionally unstable: rho=-0.383 (p=0.059 uncorrected) at d'=10 reverses sign at d'>=20 (d'=20: rho=+0.048, d'=30: rho=+0.299, d'=50: rho=+0.197). Bonferroni-corrected p=0.236. Sign reversal is a qualitative failure, not a sensitivity issue.
findings.suggestion: Acknowledge dimension instability as a confound. Do not cherry-pick d'=10 as primary dimension.

findings.description: Post-hoc power analysis (Section claims 'approximately 20% power to detect medium effect size') is methodologically questionable. Power should be computed before the experiment, not after observing null results to explain them away.
findings.suggestion: Remove post-hoc power analysis. If power is low, acknowledge this as a limitation: 'n=26 features provides insufficient power to detect medium effects, so null results are inconclusive.'

findings.description: Feature U (24.2% absorption, 100% steering) is n=1 case evidence overgeneralized to conclude 'absorption is benign.' One feature's behavior does not establish general principle. High-absorption features H (19.0%) and S (16.0%) show steering success of 0.55 and 0.65, respectively.
findings.suggestion: Remove the generalization. Report Feature U's result as a single case observation, not evidence for general benignity.

findings.description: MCC~0.21 across ALL variants including Random control suggests Hungarian matching yields chance-level recovery regardless of training. The paper acknowledges MCC failure but does not address implication: absorption reduction may reflect sparsity-induced suppression rather than genuine hierarchical feature recovery.
findings.suggestion: If MCC is at chance level for Random SAEs, the entire matching pipeline may be invalid. This needs investigation before claiming any meaningful comparisons.

findings.description: H2-H4 falsified (near-zero correlations, p>0.86) yet paper frames this as 'reframing' contribution rather than acknowledging experiments may be underpowered or wrong measurement. With n=26, power is low.
findings.suggestion: Acknowledge power limitations directly. 'Our n=26 features may lack power to detect medium effects. Results should be interpreted as inconclusive, not as evidence of no effect.'

findings.description: Title-abstract mismatch: title emphasizes 'Local Inhibition Graph' but graph predictions are falsified. The abstract and introduction claim the graph is a contribution but results show it predicts nothing.
findings.suggestion: If the contribution is the LCA-theoretical framework, retitle accordingly. Do not claim the graph is a contribution if it failed.

findings.description: H8 claim (r=+0.12, p=0.55 for total incoming inhibition vs absorption rate) has no verifiable data source. The h6_inhibition_graph.json does not contain this statistic.
findings.suggestion: Provide data source or remove specific numbers.

metric_gaming_risks: Delta-corrected steering isolates absorption-specific effects but the correction uses random baseline which may not match the specific absorption phenomenon
Precision=1.0 universally could indicate the probing task is too easy, not that absorption doesn't affect selectivity
The single significant result (H1b p=0.028) appears only with delta-correction and only at layer 8, suggesting possible p-hacking across conditions
novelty_risks: The LCA-SAE structural correspondence (G=W_dec^T W_dec) is a mathematical identity for tied-weight SAEs. The untied-weight case is 'approximate.' Given modern SAEs use untied weights, the core theoretical contribution may not apply to the actual systems being studied.

The 'local inhibition graph' approach was explored in the SAE literature (e.g., Bricken et al., Cunningham et al.) with similar top-k neighbor constructions. The specific graph construction is not clearly novel.
Competitive suppression as an explanation for absorption was hypothesized in the original Chanin et al. paper. This paper provides theoretical grounding but no new empirical validation.
reproducibility_gaps: n=26 features is too small for reliable correlation analysis (detectable effect r>0.5 only)
Single model (GPT-2 Small) limits generalization to larger models
Single SAE variant (res-jb) limits generalization to other architectures
First-letter features are a shallow hierarchy; semantic hierarchies may behave differently
Homeostatic rebalancing (H10) was deferred, so the proposed repair cannot be evaluated
Random SAE baseline comparison used different L0 values (unmatched comparison)
